channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope enters fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia on positive trajectory
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis entered the fifth week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia on Saturday with signs of recovery continuing on a positive trajectory. The Vatican announced Friday that it would provide medical updates less frequently, in what it called a positive development.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia and Ukraine launch aerial attacks amid proposed ceasefire talks
item.description: Russia and Ukraine traded heavy aerial blows overnight Saturday, with both sides reporting more than 100 enemy drones over their respective territories.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has been killed, Iraqi prime minister says
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — The head of the Islamic State in Iraq and Syria has been killed in Iraq in an operation by members of the Iraqi national intelligence service along with U.S.-led coalition forces, the Iraqi prime minister announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cuba suffers another massive power outage leaving millions in the dark
item.description: Cuba has suffered a massive power outage affecting Havana and the country’s provinces that has left millions in the dark.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Thousands converging on Serbia's capital for a major rally that tests Vucic's populist government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people swarmed the streets in Serbia's capital Belgrade on Friday, staging a joyful prelude to a major anti-government rally on the weekend and dealing a blow to populist President Aleksandar Vucic.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Killing site in Jalisco, Mexico just the latest in a long series of gruesome discoveries
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Prosecutors in western Mexico confirmed this week the discovery of hundreds of clothing items and bone fragments by a group of people searching for relatives at a previously known cartel training site, exposing major shortcomings in the original investigation.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA VERZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Most Americans disapprove of President Trump’s approach to the country's relationship with Russia and his handling of the situation in Ukraine, according to a new survey published on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: U.S. military intervention versus cartels "impossible," says Mexico's president
item.description: "He said everything’s on the table, well no, not everything is on the table, nor on the chair, nor on the floor, nor anywhere, not that.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, WGN 9

item.title: US envoy is taking Putin's comments on Ukraine ceasefire proposal to Trump, Kremlin official says
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with U.S. envoy Steve Witkoff to discuss details of the American proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Ukraine, asking him to convey Moscow's thoughts to Washington, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it will release a US-Israeli hostage and 4 bodies, but Israel expresses immediate doubt
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Palestinian militant group Hamas said Friday that it has accepted a proposal from mediators to release one living American-Israeli hostage and the bodies of four dual nationals who had died in captivity.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Waltz: Russia, Ukraine peace deal likely to include Donbas region cession
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz said Ukraine likely would have to cede some of its Donbas region to Russia if it wants a long-term peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: UN report warns Iran is stepping up electronic surveillance of women to enforce headscarf laws
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Iran is increasingly relying on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country's mandatory headscarf in public, as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Syrian Druze make a rare visit to Israeli-controlled Golan Heights
item.description: MAJDAL SHAMS, Golan Heights (AP) — Dozens of clerics and others from Syria’s minority Druze community crossed into the Israeli-controlled side of the Golan Heights Friday for the first time in decades.
item.dc:creator: BY ALON BERNSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China, Russia and Iran call for end to US sanctions on Iran and the restart of nuclear talks
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Representatives of China, Russia and Iran called Friday for an end to U.S. sanctions on Iran over its rapidly advancing nuclear program and a restart to multinational talks on the issue. The talks are the latest attempt to broach the matter and come after U.S.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN report accuses Israel of sexual violence against Palestinians. Netanyahu claims anti-Israel bias
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — United Nations-backed human rights experts on Thursday accused Israel of “the systematic use of sexual, reproductive and other gender-based violence” in its war with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sales of US goods 'rapidly dropping' at Canadian grocery stores
item.description: Canadians are buying fewer products made in the U.S. in an effort to brace for the impact of President Trump’s sweeping tariffs, which are set to cause prices to skyrocket across a range of products.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump 'hopes' Putin agrees to ceasefire as Moscow signals no truce yet
item.description: President Donald Trump said he "hopes" Russia agrees to a ceasefire deal after Ukraine accepted the terms earlier this week and warned it would be a "very disappointing moment for the world" otherwise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump signals cautious optimism on Russia accepting Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump on Thursday said he hoped Russia would “do the right thing” as his administration presses for a ceasefire agreement between Moscow and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Poland calls on US to place nukes within its borders amid Russia threat
item.description: Polish President Duda has once again called on the US to place its nuclear arms within its borders to better deter Russia, mirroring talks with France amid security concerns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia presents US with demands that need to be met before ending Ukraine war: report
item.description: Russia has reportedly laid out its demands for ending the war in Ukraine and they mirror previous requests made to Kyiv, the U.S. and NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia hits Zelenskyy's hometown as Ukraine signals it's ready for peace
item.description: As Ukraine appeared to pivot towards peace, Russia launched a deadly attack on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown of Kryvvi Rih.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search for Sudiksha Konanki, missing Pitt student, continues in Dominican Republic
item.description: Sudiksha Konanki was last seen early Thursday morning on a beach near Punta Cana. Police have spoken with a man allegedly with her at the time.
item.dc:creator: Nick Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: The leader said new tariffs are scheduled to be implemented Monday in an effort to push back on Trump’s international economic measures.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: See it: Activists vandalize Trump's Scotland golf resort
item.description: Pro-Palestinian activists said Saturday they vandalized one of Trump's golf resorts in Scotland in response to his proposal to empty the Gaza Strip of its Palestinian population.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In his own words: Pope Francis' views on resigning changed over time
item.description: ROME (AP) — As he recovers from pneumonia in the hospital, Pope Francis has increasingly handed off his day-to-day responsibilities to cardinals as questions swirl about the near and long-term future of his papacy.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's impeached President Yoon released from prison
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol was released from prison on Saturday, a day after a Seoul court canceled his arrest to allow him to stand trial for rebellion without being detained.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9
